keyPoints: A database is a collection of related data.
A database has multiple tables, each with its own schema.
Primary keys are used to uniquely identify each record.
Foreign keys are used to link tables together.
Normalization is the process of organizing data into tables.
Denormalization is the process of combining data into a single table.
Indexing improves query speed by providing a quick way to locate data.
Views are virtual tables that are based on the result of a query.
Stored procedures are pre-written code that can be executed on demand.
Transactions ensure data consistency by performing multiple operations as a single unit.
